sections.title: Context
sections.content:
This repository is the first self-referential test bed for RAGit.
The local developer workflow should run the CLI without a global install.
Recent repository changes added a `pnpm ragit` script that executes `tsx src/cli.ts`.
Recent repository changes also made `pnpm build` optional for repository-local usage.

sections.title: Decision
sections.content:
RAGit local execution in this repository uses `pnpm ragit <command>` as the canonical runner.
`pnpm build` remains optional and is used only when `dist/` artifacts or packaged CLI validation are needed.

sections.title: Consequences
sections.content:
The round-1 test bed must use `pnpm ragit` in all operational commands.
Golden queries should explicitly confirm that RAGit can run locally without building `dist/`.
Setup guidance should explain that `pnpm build` is optional, not mandatory, for repository-local usage.

sections.title: Bounded Context
sections.content: The bounded context is commit-bound retrieval for repository-local AI working knowledge.

sections.title: Commit
sections.content:
A Commit is the source-of-truth boundary shared by Git and RAGit.
Git stores the code state of the commit.
RAGit stores the knowledge snapshot bound to the same commit SHA.

sections.title: Snapshot
sections.content:
A Snapshot is the retrieval state recorded in `.ragit/manifest/<commit-sha>.json`.
It references the set of documents and chunks that define searchable knowledge for a commit.

sections.title: Document
sections.content:
A Document is a typed markdown artifact such as ADR, PRD, SRS, SPEC, Plan, DDD, Glossary, or PBD.
In the test bed, each document belongs to the `testbed/docs/` corpus.

sections.title: Chunk
sections.content:
A Chunk is a searchable section fragment derived from a document.
Query ranking combines vector similarity and keyword score over chunks.

sections.title: Query
sections.content:
A Query asks for knowledge at HEAD or at a specific snapshot SHA.
The result is meaningful only if the bound snapshot reflects the intended commit state.

sections.title: Control Plane
sections.content: The control plane is the `.ragit/` area that stores config, guide files, manifests, store, cache, and hooks.

sections.title: Isolated Ingest
sections.content: Isolated ingest is an ingest run limited to `testbed/docs/**/*.md`.

sections.title: Mixed Ingest
sections.content: Mixed ingest is an ingest run across the whole repository with `pnpm ragit ingest --all`.

sections.title: Manifest
sections.content:
A manifest in the round-1 test bed is a snapshot index file stored at `.ragit/manifest/<commit-sha>.json`.
The manifest binds documents and chunks to one commit SHA.

sections.title: Snapshot
sections.content:
A snapshot in the round-1 test bed is the commit-bound retrieval state used by `query` and `context pack`.
The snapshot answers what knowledge should be used at a specific commit.

sections.title: Context Pack
sections.content: A context pack is a budget-limited collection of relevant retrieval hits prepared for an AI agent task.

sections.title: Hook Validation
sections.content: Hook validation is verification that `post-commit` automatically creates a new manifest after a document-only commit.

sections.title: Time-Travel Query
sections.content: A time-travel query is a `query --at <sha>` call that reconstructs knowledge for an older commit state.

sections.title: Implementation Scope
sections.content: This PBD describes how the round-1 self-referential test bed moves through phases and how each phase binds to the next.

sections.title: Phase Topology
sections.content:
Phase 1 is baseline verification.
Phase 2 is `init` normalization of the `.ragit` control plane.
Phase 3 is isolated corpus materialization under `testbed/docs/`.
Phase 4 is isolated ingest and golden retrieval validation.
Phase 5 is hook validation through a document-only commit.
Phase 6 is time-travel validation with `query --at <sha>`.
Phase 7 is mixed ingest validation across the whole repository.

sections.title: Binding Map
sections.content:
`init` binds `AGENTS.md` to `.ragit/guide/guide-index.json`.
`ingest` binds documents to chunks and binds chunks to a manifest at a commit SHA.
`query` binds a user question to a specific snapshot.
`context pack` binds a goal to a selected subset of retrieval hits.
`post-commit` binds a new commit event to automatic incremental ingest.

sections.title: Interaction Paths
sections.content:
Git produces a commit.
RAGit observes the commit SHA.
RAGit indexes changed markdown knowledge.
RAGit returns commit-bound knowledge for queries and agent context.

sections.title: Failure and Drift Points
sections.content:
If `.ragit/**` is assumed to be searchable corpus, retrieval will fail because current ingest ignores `.ragit/**`.
If hook validation commits unrelated markdown changes, isolated validation noise increases.
If a manifest is missing for a target SHA, `query --at <sha>` cannot reconstruct that state.

sections.title: Observability Notes
sections.content: The most important observations are manifest count, top hit path, top-3 stability, and differences between isolated and mixed ingest.

sections.title: Milestones
sections.content:
M1. Normalize `.ragit/` with `pnpm ragit init`.
M2. Materialize the isolated test corpus under `testbed/docs/`.
M3. Run isolated ingest and golden queries.
M4. Validate hook automation and commit-bound time travel.
M5. Run mixed ingest and publish the round-1 report.

sections.title: Work Breakdown
sections.content:
Task 1. Verify baseline with `pnpm test` and `pnpm ragit --help`.
Task 2. Create eight documents and a golden query file.
Task 3. Run isolated validation and capture acceptance metrics.
Task 4. Run hook validation with a single-document commit.
Task 5. Run time-travel validation with `--at <sha>`.
Task 6. Run mixed validation and summarize noise patterns.

sections.title: Functional Requirements
sections.content:
FR-001: The test bed must start from `pnpm ragit init --yes --output json`.
FR-002: The test bed must create eight test documents, one per supported document type.
FR-003: The isolated ingest phase must use `pnpm ragit ingest --files "testbed/docs/**/*.md"`.
FR-004: The test bed must validate `query`, `context pack`, `hooks install`, and `query --at <sha>`.
FR-005: The test bed must record results in a round-1 report document.

sections.title: Non-Functional Requirements
sections.content:
NFR-001: The test corpus must use only facts already present in this repository.
NFR-002: The control plane stays in `.ragit/`, while searchable corpus stays outside `.ragit/`.
NFR-003: Golden queries should be specific enough to be checked repeatedly across isolated and mixed ingest runs.

sections.title: Scope
sections.content:
This specification defines the execution contract for the first RAGit self-referential test bed.
It covers initialization, isolated ingest, golden query validation, hook validation, time-travel validation, and mixed ingest validation.

sections.title: Functional Requirements
sections.content:
The round-1 operator runs RAGit locally with `pnpm ragit`.
The round-1 operator does not require `pnpm build` for repository-local usage.
The isolated ingest command is `pnpm ragit ingest --files "testbed/docs/**/*.md"`.
The mixed ingest command is `pnpm ragit ingest --all`.

sections.title: Interfaces and Contracts
sections.content:
The isolated test corpus lives under `testbed/docs/`.
The control plane lives under `.ragit/`.
`.ragit` is not itself a searchable document source because the current ingest implementation ignores `.ragit/**`.

sections.title: State and Flow
sections.content:
State A is the baseline test-bed corpus commit.
State B is the hook validation commit with a meaningful document update.
State C is the time-travel validation commit with a second meaningful document update.
Queries without `--at` use the latest available snapshot.
Queries with `--at <sha>` must reproduce the knowledge state of the referenced commit.

sections.title: Acceptance Criteria
sections.content:
- At least 8 of 10 golden queries return the expected top-1 hit during isolated validation.
- All 10 golden queries return the expected answer within top-3 during isolated validation.
- Mixed validation keeps at least 7 of 10 golden queries within top-3.

sections.title: Goal
sections.content:
The product goal of this round is to use the RAGit repository as a self-referential test bed.
The test bed should prove that RAGit can initialize itself, ingest project knowledge, answer questions, and reconstruct commit-bound context.

sections.title: User Value
sections.content:
Users should understand that Git and RAGit solve adjacent but different problems.
Git version-controls source code states.
RAGit version-controls AI-working knowledge states bound to the same commit history.

Users should be able to explain the product purpose and core value of RAGit from repository-local knowledge.
Users should understand that local repository usage runs through `pnpm ragit`, while `pnpm build` is optional for repository-local execution.
Users should understand the difference between Git code history and RAGit commit-bound knowledge history.
Users should understand that PBD explains the phase topology and binding structure of the round-1 test bed.

sections.title: Success Criteria
sections.content:
The isolated test corpus should return correct answers for golden queries.
Time-travel queries with `--at <sha>` should reflect old and new document states.
Mixed ingest with repository-wide documents should still keep core test-bed questions inside the top three hits.
